title: smallanthus latisquamus
intro: Smallanthus latisquamus, also known as Jerusalem artichoke, is a perennial sunflower species native to North America, characterized by its edible tubers and edible flowers. The medicinal properties of Smallanthus latisquamus include its inulin content, which has prebiotic properties, and its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of consuming Smallanthus latisquamus include supporting gut health, lowering cholesterol levels, and aiding in weight management. Smallanthus latisquamus can be prepared as herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and can also be dried and used as a dietary supplement, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
